   889→  defp assign_table_artifact(socket, artifact, params) do
   890→    artifact_path = Map.get(artifact, :path, "unknown")
   891→    path_hash = :md5 |> :crypto.hash(artifact_path) |> Base.encode16() |> String.slice(0, 8)
   892→    page_param = "bundle-size-analysis-table-page-#{path_hash}"
   893→
   894→    bundle_size_analysis_sunburst_chart_table_page =
   895→      String.to_integer(params[page_param] || "1")
   896→
   897→    table_page_size = 5
   898→
   899→    artifact_children =
   900→      Enum.map(
   901→        artifact.children,
   902→        &add_collapsed_to_artifact(&1, socket)
   903→      )
   904→
   905→    current_page_children =
   906→      Enum.slice(
   907→        artifact_children,
   908→        (bundle_size_analysis_sunburst_chart_table_page - 1) * table_page_size,
   909→        table_page_size
   910→      )
   911→
   912→    bundle_size_analysis_sunburst_chart_table_page_count =
   913→      max(div(length(artifact_children), table_page_size), 1)
   914→
   915→    socket
   916→    |> assign(:bundle_size_analysis_sunburst_chart_table_artifact, artifact)
   917→    |> assign(
   918→      :bundle_size_analysis_sunburst_chart_table_page,
   919→      bundle_size_analysis_sunburst_chart_table_page
   920→    )
   921→    |> assign(
   922→      :bundle_size_analysis_sunburst_chart_table_page_count,
   923→      bundle_size_analysis_sunburst_chart_table_page_count
   924→    )
   925→    |> assign(
   926→      :bundle_size_analysis_sunburst_chart_table_current_page_artifacts,
   927→      current_page_children
   928→    )
   929→    |> assign(
   930→      :bundle_size_analysis_sunburst_chart_table_page_param,
   931→      page_param
   932→    )
   933→  end
   934→
   935→  defp add_collapsed_to_artifact(artifact, %{assigns: %{artifacts_by_path: artifacts_by_path}} = _socket) do
   936→    path = Map.get(artifact, :path)
   937→    full_artifact = path && Map.get(artifacts_by_path, path)
   938→
   939→    if full_artifact do
   940→      collapsed = Map.get(full_artifact, :collapsed?, false)
   941→      Map.put(artifact, :collapsed?, collapsed)
   942→    else
   943→      artifact
   944→    end
   945→  end
   946→
   947→  defp remove_pagination_params(params) do
   948→    params
   949→    |> Enum.reject(fn {key, _value} -> String.starts_with?(key, "bundle-size-analysis-table-page-") end)
   950→    |> Map.new()
   951→  end
   952→
   953→  defp build_artifact_for_table(artifact, duplicates) do
   954→    duplicate_shasums = MapSet.new(duplicates, & &1.shasum)
   955→
   956→    %{
   957→      path: artifact.path,
   958→      name: artifact.path |> String.split("/") |> List.last(),
   959→      value: artifact.size,
   960→      id: artifact.id,
   961→      artifact_id: artifact.artifact_id,
   962→      artifact_type: artifact.artifact_type || :directory,
   963→      children: build_artifact_children(artifact.children || [], duplicate_shasums)
   964→    }
   965→  end
   966→
   967→  defp build_root_table_artifact(bundle, base_path, duplicates) do
   968→    duplicate_shasums = MapSet.new(duplicates, & &1.shasum)
   969→
   970→    %{
   971→      path: base_path,
   972→      name: bundle.name,
   973→      value: bundle.install_size,
   974→      artifact_type: :directory,
   975→      children: build_artifact_children(bundle.artifacts, duplicate_shasums)
   976→    }
   977→  end
   978→
   979→  defp build_artifact_children(artifacts, duplicate_shasums) do
   980→    artifacts
   981→    |> Enum.map(
   982→      &%{
   983→        value: &1.size,
   984→        name: &1.path |> String.split("/") |> List.last(),
   985→        artifact_type: &1.artifact_type,
   986→        artifact_id: &1.artifact_id,
   987→        path: &1.path,
   988→        duplicate?: &1.shasum && MapSet.member?(duplicate_shasums, &1.shasum)
   989→      }
   990→    )
   991→    |> Enum.sort_by(& &1.value, :desc)
   992→  end

1→defmodule TuistWeb.API.Schemas.BundleArtifact do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  The schema for the bundle artifact.
     4→  """

     7→  alias Tuist.Bundles.Artifact

     9→  require OpenApiSpex

    12→    title: "BundleArtifact",
    13→    description: "A bundle artifact schema",
    14→    type: :object,
    15→    properties: %{
    16→      artifact_type: %Schema{
    17→        type: :string,
    18→        description: "The type of artifact",
    19→        enum: Ecto.Enum.values(Artifact, :artifact_type)
    20→      },
    21→      path: %Schema{
    22→        type: :string,
    23→        description: "The path to the artifact relative to the root of the bundle."
    24→      },

    27→        description: "The size of the artifact in bytes"
    28→      },
    29→      shasum: %Schema{

    31→        description: "The SHA checksum of the artifact"
    32→      },
    33→      children: %Schema{
    34→        type: :array,
    35→        description: "Nested child artifacts, for example for artifacts that represent a directory.",
    36→        items: %Reference{"$ref": "#/components/schemas/BundleArtifact"}
    37→      }
    38→    },
    39→    required: [:artifact_type, :path, :size, :shasum]
    40→  })
    41→end

1→defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareBundles do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Guides you through comparing two bundles to identify size changes across the artifact tree. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}. They are not needed if base or head is a dashboard URL.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :prompt
     7→
     8→  alias Anubis.Server.Response
     9→  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.PromptSupport
    10→
    11→  schema do
    12→    field :account_handle, :string, description: "The account handle (organization or user)."
    13→
    14→    field :project_handle, :string, description: "The project handle."
    15→
    16→    field :base, :string,
    17→      description:
    18→        "Base bundle: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. " <>
    19→          "Defaults to the latest bundle on the project's default branch when omitted."
    20→
    21→    field :head, :string,
    22→      description:
    23→        "Head bundle: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. " <>
    24→          "This is the bundle you want to evaluate. When provided without a base, " <>
    25→          "the base defaults to the latest bundle on the project's default branch."
    26→  end
    27→
    28→  @impl true
    29→  def get_messages(arguments, frame) do
    30→    base = Map.get(arguments, :base)
    31→    head = Map.get(arguments, :head)
    32→    {account_handle, project_handle} = PromptSupport.resolve_project_handles(arguments)
    33→    default_branch = PromptSupport.resolve_default_branch(account_handle, project_handle)
    34→
    35→    response =
    36→      Response.user_message(Response.prompt(), %{
    37→        "type" => "text",
    38→        "text" => prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch)
    39→      })
    40→
    41→    {:reply, response, frame}
    42→  end
    43→
    44→  defp prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch) do
    45→    resolution = resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch)
    46→
    47→    """
    48→    # Compare Bundles
    49→
    50→    Use MCP tools to fetch two bundles and compare their size and artifact composition.
    51→
    52→    ## Available MCP tools
    53→
    54→    - **list_projects**: List all accessible projects.
    55→    - **list_bundles**: List bundles for a project (supports git_branch filter).
    56→    - **get_bundle**: Get bundle metadata (sizes, version, platforms).
    57→    - **list_bundle_artifacts**: List artifacts for a bundle. Use `parent_artifact_id` to drill into directories.
    58→
    59→    ## Workflow
    60→
    61→    #{resolution}
    62→
    63→    ### 2. Compare top-level sizes
    64→
    65→    Compare these fields between the two bundles:
    66→    - **install_size**: The installed size on device. Flag increases over 5%.
    67→    - **download_size**: The download size from the store. Flag increases over 5%.
    68→    - **version**: Note version changes.
    69→    - **supported_platforms**: Note platform changes.
    70→
    71→    ### 3. Compare artifact trees
    72→
    73→    Use `list_bundle_artifacts` for both bundles to get top-level artifacts. For each artifact:
    74→    - Match artifacts by `path` between base and head.
    75→    - Calculate size delta for each matched artifact.
    76→    - Identify new artifacts in head that don't exist in base.
    77→    - Identify removed artifacts that were in base but not in head.
    78→
    79→    **Always drill into directories.** For every artifact with `has_children: true`, call `list_bundle_artifacts` again with `parent_artifact_id` set to that artifact's `id` for both base and head bundles. Continue recursing until you reach leaf artifacts (files). This is essential to pinpoint which specific files drove size changes.
    80→
    81→    Sort artifacts by absolute size increase to find the biggest contributors.
    82→
    83→    ### Summary format
    84→
    85→    Produce a structured summary with:
    86→    1. **Overall**: install_size delta, download_size delta, verdict (acceptable/concerning).
    87→    2. **Top size increases**: up to 10 artifacts with the largest size growth.
    88→    3. **New artifacts**: artifacts added in the head bundle.
    89→    4. **Removed artifacts**: artifacts removed from the base bundle.
    90→    5. **Recommendations**: actionable next steps. Since the developer is likely on the head branch, suggest concrete local fixes (e.g., "remove unused assets in Resources/", "optimize image at path/to/large.png").
    91→    """
    92→  end
    93→

    95→    branch = default_branch || "main"
    96→
    97→    project_line =
    98→      if account_handle && project_handle,
    99→        do: "Project: `#{account_handle}/#{project_handle}`.\n",
   100→        else: ""
   101→

   106→
   107→        _ ->

   109→      end

   117→
   118→        _ ->

   120→      end

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a summary document with all the findings:

## Summary of Existing MCP Tools Related to Bundles

I've thoroughly explored the Tuist server codebase and found the existing bundle-related MCP tools and infrastructure. Here's what exists:

### 1. MCP Tool Definitions

**Located in:** `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/components/tools/`

Three bundle-related tools are currently implemented:

#### **list_bundles.ex**
- **Purpose:** List bundles for a project with pagination and filtering
- **Parameters:**
  - `account_handle` (required): Organization or user handle
  - `project_handle` (required): Project handle
  - `git_branch` (optional): Filter by git branch
  - `page` (optional): Page number (default: 1)
  - `page_size` (optional): Results per page (default: 20, max: 100)
- **Returns:** Array of bundle objects with metadata:
  - `id`, `name`, `app_bundle_id`, `version`, `type` (ipa, app, xcarchive, aab, apk)
  - `supported_platforms` (ios, ios_simulator, tvos, tvos_simulator, watchos, watchos_simulator, visionos, visionos_simulator, macos, android)
  - `install_size`, `download_size`
  - `git_branch`, `git_commit_sha`, `inserted_at`
  - Pagination metadata

#### **get_bundle.ex**
- **Purpose:** Get detailed information about a specific bundle
- **Parameters:**
  - `bundle_id` (required): The ID of the bundle (can also be a Tuist dashboard URL)
- **Returns:** Single bundle object with all metadata fields (same as list_bundles but without pagination)

#### **list_bundle_artifacts.ex**
- **Purpose:** List artifacts for a bundle with hierarchical drilling capability
- **Parameters:**
  - `bundle_id` (required): The ID of the bundle (can be a Tuist dashboard URL)
  - `parent_artifact_id` (optional): Drill into nested artifacts; omit to list top-level artifacts
- **Returns:** Array of artifact objects with:
  - `id`, `artifact_type` (directory, file, font, binary, localization, asset, unknown)
  - `path` (relative to bundle root)
  - `size` (in bytes)
  - `shasum` (SHA checksum for deduplication)
  - `has_children` (boolean: true if artifact_type is directory)

### 2. Data Structures

#### **Bundle Schema** (`/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/bundles/bundle.ex`)
```elixir
schema "bundles" do
  field :app_bundle_id, :string
  field :name, :string
  field :install_size, :integer
  field :download_size, :integer
  field :git_branch
  field :git_commit_sha
  field :git_ref
  field :supported_platforms, {:array, Ecto.Enum} # 10 platform options
  field :version, :string
  field :type, Ecto.Enum # 5 bundle types
  belongs_to :project, Tuist.Projects.Project
  belongs_to :uploaded_by_account, Tuist.Accounts.Account
  has_many :artifacts, Artifact
  timestamps(type: :utc_datetime)
end
```

#### **Artifact Schema** (`/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/bundles/artifact.ex`)
```elixir
schema "artifacts" do
  field :artifact_type, Ecto.Enum # 7 types: directory, file, font, binary, localization, asset, unknown
  field :path, :string
  field :size, :integer
  field :shasum, :string
  belongs_to :bundle, Tuist.Bundles.Bundle
  belongs_to :artifact, __MODULE__ # self-referential for parent-child relationships
  has_many :children, __MODULE__
  timestamps(type: :utc_datetime)
end
```

### 3. Core Implementation: `list_bundle_artifacts`

**Location:** `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/bundles.ex` (lines 88-105)

```elixir
def list_bundle_artifacts(bundle_id, opts \\ []) do
  parent_artifact_id = Keyword.get(opts, :parent_artifact_id)

  query =
    from(a in Artifact,
      where: a.bundle_id == ^bundle_id,
      order_by: [asc: a.path]
    )

  query =
    if parent_artifact_id do
      where(query, [a], a.artifact_id == ^parent_artifact_id)
    else
      where(query, [a], is_nil(a.artifact_id))
    end

  Repo.all(query)
end
```

**How it works:**
- Returns a flat list of artifacts (no nested structure) filtered by parent_artifact_id
- Top-level artifacts have `nil` parent IDs
- Child artifacts have the parent's artifact ID stored in `artifact_id` field
- Results are sorted by path in ascending order
- The MCP tool wrapper adds `has_children` flag based on artifact_type == :directory

### 4. Web UI Bundle Overview/Breakdown

**Location:** `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist_web/live/bundle_live.ex`

The web UI shows:

1. **Sunburst Chart** (interactive visualization)
   - Shows artifact tree hierarchically with visual coloring by artifact type
   - Supports filter by artifact type
   - Supports navigation up/down the tree

2. **File Breakdown Tab**
   - Filters: artifact type, size (KB), space usage (%)
   - Sortable by: path, size
   - Shows only leaf artifacts (files)
   - Paginated at 20 results per page

3. **Module Breakdown Tab**
   - Filters: frameworks ending with `.framework`
   - Sortable by: name, size
   - Paginated at 20 results per page

4. **Duplicates Detection**
   - Finds artifacts with same shasum
   - Calculates wasted space from duplicates
   - Marks duplicates with special color in sunburst chart

5. **Size Analytics**
   - Tracks install_size and download_size over time
   - Compares to previous bundle on same branch

### 5. Comparison Prompt

**Location:** `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/components/prompts/compare_bundles.ex`

The `compare_bundles` prompt guides users through:
- Resolving two bundles (by ID, URL, or branch)
- Comparing install_size and download_size (flag >5% changes)
- Recursively drilling into artifact trees to find what changed
- Generating structured comparison summary with top changes, new/removed artifacts, and recommendations

### Key Design Points

1. **Hierarchical Storage:** Artifacts use self-referential parent-child relationships via `artifact_id` field
2. **Lazy Loading:** The API returns flat lists that clients drill into using `parent_artifact_id`
3. **Deduplication:** Artifacts with identical content (shasum) are tracked for dedup analysis
4. **Collapsed Artifacts:** The web UI collapses large artifact lists (50+ items) to avoid overwhelming users
5. **Unknown Artifacts:** During creation, if children's total size < parent size, an "Unknown" artifact is created for the delta

---

**File Paths for Reference:**
- Bundle tools: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/components/tools/`
- Bundle context: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/bundles.ex`
- Bundle schemas: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/bundles/{bundle,artifact}.ex`
- Web UI: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/bundle_live.ex`
- Tests: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/test/tuist/mcp/components/tools/bundle_tools_test.exs`

```elixir
  has_many :children, __MODULE__
  timestamps(type: :utc_datetime)
end
```

### 3. Core Implementation: `list_bundle_artifacts`

**Location:** `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/bundles.ex` (lines 88-105)

```elixir
def list_bundle_artifacts(bundle_id, opts \\ []) do
  parent_artifact_id = Keyword.get(opts, :parent_artifact_id)

  query =
    from(a in Artifact,
      where: a.bundle_id == ^bundle_id,
      order_by: [asc: a.path]
    )

  query =
    if parent_artifact_id do
      where(query, [a], a.artifact_id == ^parent_artifact_id)
    else
      where(query, [a], is_nil(a.artifact_id))
    end

  Repo.all(query)
end
```

**How it works:**
- Returns a flat list of artifacts (no nested structure) filtered by parent_artifact_id
- Top-level artifacts have `nil` parent IDs
- Child artifacts have the parent's artifact ID stored in `artifact_id` field
- Results are sorted by path in ascending order
- The MCP tool wrapper adds `has_children` flag based on artifact_type == :directory

### 4. Web UI Bundle Overview/Breakdown

**Location:** `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist_web/live/bundle_live.ex`

The web UI shows:

1. **Sunburst Chart** (interactive visualization)
   - Shows artifact tree hierarchically with visual coloring by artifact type
   - Supports filter by artifact type
   - Supports navigation up/down the tree

2. **File Breakdown Tab**
   - Filters: artifact type, size (KB), space usage (%)
   - Sortable by: path, size
   - Shows only leaf artifacts (files)
   - Paginated at 20 results per page

3. **Module Breakdown Tab**
   - Filters: frameworks ending with `.framework`
   - Sortable by: name, size
   - Paginated at 20 results per page

4. **Duplicates Detection**
   - Finds artifacts with same shasum
   - Calculates wasted space from duplicates
   - Marks duplicates with special color in sunburst chart

5. **Size Analytics**
   - Tracks install_size and download_size over time
   - Compares to previous bundle on same branch

### 5. Comparison Prompt

**Location:** `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/components/prompts/compare_bundles.ex`

The `compare_bundles` prompt guides users through:
- Resolving two bundles (by ID, URL, or branch)
- Comparing install_size and download_size (flag >5% changes)
- Recursively drilling into artifact trees to find what changed
- Generating structured comparison summary with top changes, new/removed artifacts, and recommendations

### Key Design Points

1. **Hierarchical Storage:** Artifacts use self-referential parent-child relationships via `artifact_id` field
2. **Lazy Loading:** The API returns flat lists that clients drill into using `parent_artifact_id`
3. **Deduplication:** Artifacts with identical content (shasum) are tracked for dedup analysis
4. **Collapsed Artifacts:** The web UI collapses large artifact lists (50+ items) to avoid overwhelming users
5. **Unknown Artifacts:** During creation, if children's total size < parent size, an "Unknown" artifact is created for the delta

---

**File Paths for Reference:**
- Bundle tools: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/mcp/components/tools/`
- Bundle context: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/bundles.ex`
- Bundle schemas: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist/bundles/{bundle,artifact}.ex`
- Web UI: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/live/bundle_live.ex`
- Tests: `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/test/tuist/mcp/components/tools/bundle_tools_test.exs`

Now I understand the full picture. The reviewer wants two changes:

1. Instead of instructing the agent to recursively drill into the artifact tree (which overwhelms LLM context), we should add a new tool that returns a flat list of all artifacts (like the file/module breakdown in the UI)
2. The prompt should instruct the agent to fetch the full tree as JSON and save it locally

Let me look at what the web UI does for the flat file breakdown and create a new tool + update the prompt.

defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareBundles do
  @moduledoc """
  Guides you through comparing two bundles to identify size changes across the artifact tree. The account_handle and project_handle can be extracted from a Tuist dashboard URL: https://tuist.dev/{account_handle}/{project_handle}. They are not needed if base or head is a dashboard URL.
  """

  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :prompt

  alias Anubis.Server.Response
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.PromptSupport

  schema do
    field :account_handle, :string, description: "The account handle (organization or user)."

    field :project_handle, :string, description: "The project handle."

    field :base, :string,
      description:
        "Base bundle: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. " <>
          "Defaults to the latest bundle on the project's default branch when omitted."

    field :head, :string,
      description:
        "Head bundle: an ID, a Tuist dashboard URL, or a branch name. " <>
          "This is the bundle you want to evaluate. When provided without a base, " <>
          "the base defaults to the latest bundle on the project's default branch."
  end

  @impl true
  def get_messages(arguments, frame) do
    base = Map.get(arguments, :base)
    head = Map.get(arguments, :head)
    {account_handle, project_handle} = PromptSupport.resolve_project_handles(arguments)
    default_branch = PromptSupport.resolve_default_branch(account_handle, project_handle)

    response =
      Response.user_message(Response.prompt(), %{
        "type" => "text",
        "text" => prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch)
      })

    {:reply, response, frame}
  end

  defp prompt_text(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch) do
    resolution = resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch)

    """
    # Compare Bundles

    Use MCP tools to fetch two bundles and compare their size and artifact composition.

    ## Available MCP tools

    - **list_projects**: List all accessible projects.
    - **list_bundles**: List bundles for a project (supports git_branch filter).
    - **get_bundle**: Get bundle metadata (sizes, version, platforms).
    - **list_bundle_artifacts**: List artifacts for a bundle. Use `parent_artifact_id` to drill into directories.

    ## Workflow

    #{resolution}

    ### 2. Compare top-level sizes

    Compare these fields between the two bundles:
    - **install_size**: The installed size on device. Flag increases over 5%.
    - **download_size**: The download size from the store. Flag increases over 5%.
    - **version**: Note version changes.
    - **supported_platforms**: Note platform changes.

    ### 3. Compare artifact trees

    Use `list_bundle_artifacts` for both bundles to get top-level artifacts. For each artifact:
    - Match artifacts by `path` between base and head.
    - Calculate size delta for each matched artifact.
    - Identify new artifacts in head that don't exist in base.
    - Identify removed artifacts that were in base but not in head.

    **Always drill into directories.** For every artifact with `has_children: true`, call `list_bundle_artifacts` again with `parent_artifact_id` set to that artifact's `id` for both base and head bundles. Continue recursing until you reach leaf artifacts (files). This is essential to pinpoint which specific files drove size changes.

    Sort artifacts by absolute size increase to find the biggest contributors.

    ### Summary format

    Produce a structured summary with:
    1. **Overall**: install_size delta, download_size delta, verdict (acceptable/concerning).
    2. **Top size increases**: up to 10 artifacts with the largest size growth.
    3. **New artifacts**: artifacts added in the head bundle.
    4. **Removed artifacts**: artifacts removed from the base bundle.
    5. **Recommendations**: actionable next steps. Since the developer is likely on the head branch, suggest concrete local fixes (e.g., "remove unused assets in Resources/", "optimize image at path/to/large.png").
    """
  end

  defp resolution_section(base, head, account_handle, project_handle, default_branch) do
    branch = default_branch || "main"

    project_line =
      if account_handle && project_handle,
        do: "Project: `#{account_handle}/#{project_handle}`.\n",
        else: ""

    base_instruction =
      case base do
        nil ->
          "For the **base**, use `list_bundles` with `git_branch=#{branch}` and pick the most recent bundle."

        _ ->
          "For the **base**, use `get_bundle` with `bundle_id=#{base}`."
      end

    head_instruction =
      case head do
        nil ->
          "For the **head**, detect the current git branch (e.g., run `git branch --show-current`). " <>
            "If it differs from `#{branch}`, use `list_bundles` with that branch and pick the most recent bundle. " <>
            "If it matches the base branch, ask the user which bundle to compare."

        _ ->
          "For the **head**, use `get_bundle` with `bundle_id=#{head}`."
      end

    """
    ### 1. Resolve bundles

    #{project_line}#{base_instruction}
    #{head_instruction}
    """
  end
end

defmodule Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListBundleArtifacts do
  @moduledoc """
  List artifacts for a bundle. Use parent_artifact_id to drill into nested artifacts. The bundle_id can also be a Tuist dashboard URL, e.g. https://tuist.dev/{account}/{project}/bundles/{id}.
  """

  use Anubis.Server.Component, type: :tool

  alias Anubis.Server.Response
  alias Tuist.Bundles
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.ToolSupport

  @authorization_action :read
  @authorization_category :bundle

  schema do
    field :bundle_id, :string,
      required: true,
      description: "The ID of the bundle."

    field :parent_artifact_id, :string,
      description:
        "The ID of a parent artifact to list children of. " <>
          "Omit to list top-level artifacts."
  end

  @impl true
  def execute(%{bundle_id: bundle_id} = arguments, frame) do
    parent_artifact_id = Map.get(arguments, :parent_artifact_id)

    with {:ok, bundle} <-
           ToolSupport.load_resource(
             Bundles.get_bundle(bundle_id),
             "Bundle not found: #{bundle_id}",
             frame
           ),
         {:ok, _project} <-
           ToolSupport.authorize_project_by_id(
             frame,
             bundle.project_id,
             @authorization_action,
             @authorization_category
           ) do
      opts = if parent_artifact_id, do: [parent_artifact_id: parent_artifact_id], else: []
      artifacts = Bundles.list_bundle_artifacts(bundle_id, opts)

      data = %{
        bundle_id: bundle_id,
        parent_artifact_id: parent_artifact_id,
        artifacts:
          Enum.map(artifacts, fn artifact ->
            %{
              id: artifact.id,
              artifact_type: to_string(artifact.artifact_type),
              path: artifact.path,
              size: artifact.size,
              shasum: artifact.shasum,
              has_children: artifact.artifact_type == :directory
            }
          end)
      }

      {:reply, Response.json(Response.tool(), data), frame}
    end
  end
end

1→defmodule Tuist.Bundles do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  The Bundles context.
     4→  """
     5→
     6→  import Ecto.Query
     7→
     8→  alias Tuist.Bundles.Artifact
     9→  alias Tuist.Bundles.Bundle
    10→  alias Tuist.Bundles.BundleThreshold
    11→  alias Tuist.Projects.Project
    12→  alias Tuist.Repo
    13→
    14→  @doc """
    15→  Creates a bundle with associated artifacts.
    16→  """
    17→  def create_bundle(attrs \\ %{}, opts \\ []) do
    18→    {artifacts, bundle_attrs} = Map.pop(attrs, :artifacts, [])
    19→    bundle_id = Map.fetch!(attrs, :id)
    20→
    21→    Ecto.Multi.new()
    22→    |> create_bundle_multi(bundle_attrs)
    23→    |> create_artifacts_multi(artifacts, bundle_id)
    24→    |> execute_bundle_transaction(opts)
    25→  end
    26→
    27→  defp create_bundle_multi(multi, bundle_attrs) do
    28→    Ecto.Multi.insert(multi, :bundle, Bundle.changeset(%Bundle{}, bundle_attrs))
    29→  end
    30→
    31→  defp create_artifacts_multi(multi, artifacts, bundle_id) do
    32→    Ecto.Multi.run(multi, :artifacts, fn repo, _changes ->
    33→      insert_artifacts_in_batches(repo, artifacts, bundle_id)
    34→    end)
    35→  end
    36→
    37→  defp execute_bundle_transaction(multi, opts) do
    38→    preload = Keyword.get(opts, :preload, [])
    39→
    40→    case Repo.transaction(multi) do
    41→      {:ok, %{bundle: bundle}} -> {:ok, Repo.preload(bundle, preload)}
    42→      {:error, _operation, changeset, _changes} -> {:error, changeset}
    43→    end
    44→  end
    45→
    46→  defp insert_artifacts_in_batches(repo, artifacts, bundle_id) do
    47→    artifacts
    48→    |> flatten_artifacts(bundle_id)
    49→    |> batch_insert_artifacts(repo)
    50→  end
    51→
    52→  defp batch_insert_artifacts(flattened_artifacts, repo) do
    53→    # Each artifact has ~10 fields, so 6000 artifacts per batch keeps us under 65535 params
    54→    batch_size = 6000
    55→
    56→    flattened_artifacts
    57→    |> Enum.chunk_every(batch_size)
    58→    |> Enum.reduce_while({:ok, []}, fn batch, {:ok, acc} ->
    59→      case repo.insert_all(Artifact, batch, returning: true) do
    60→        {_count, artifacts} -> {:cont, {:ok, acc ++ artifacts}}
    61→        error -> {:halt, error}
    62→      end
    63→    end)
    64→  end
    65→
    66→  @doc """
    67→  Gets a single bundle.
    68→  """
    69→  def get_bundle(id, opts \\ []) do
    70→    preload = Keyword.get(opts, :preload, [])
    71→    project_id = Keyword.get(opts, :project_id)
    72→
    73→    query =
    74→      then(
    75→        from(b in Bundle, where: b.id == ^id, preload: ^preload),
    76→        &if(is_nil(project_id), do: &1, else: where(&1, [b], b.project_id == ^project_id))
    77→      )
    78→
    79→    bundle = Repo.one(query)
    80→
    81→    if is_nil(bundle) do
    82→      {:error, :not_found}
    83→    else
    84→      {:ok, %{bundle | artifacts: bundle_artifacts(bundle)}}
    85→    end
    86→  end
    87→
    88→  def list_bundle_artifacts(bundle_id, opts \\ []) do
    89→    parent_artifact_id = Keyword.get(opts, :parent_artifact_id)
    90→
    91→    query =
    92→      from(a in Artifact,
    93→        where: a.bundle_id == ^bundle_id,
    94→        order_by: [asc: a.path]
    95→      )
    96→
    97→    query =
    98→      if parent_artifact_id do
    99→        where(query, [a], a.artifact_id == ^parent_artifact_id)
   100→      else
   101→        where(query, [a], is_nil(a.artifact_id))
   102→      end
   103→
   104→    Repo.all(query)
   105→  end
   106→
   107→  defp bundle_artifacts(%Bundle{id: id}) do
   108→    # Get all artifacts for this bundle in a single query
   109→    all_artifacts =
   110→      Repo.all(
   111→        from a in Artifact,
   112→          where: a.bundle_id == ^id,
   113→          select: %{
   114→            id: a.id,
   115→            artifact_type: a.artifact_type,
   116→            path: a.path,
   117→            size: a.size,
   118→            shasum: a.shasum,
   119→            artifact_id: a.artifact_id,
   120→            bundle_id: a.bundle_id,
   121→            inserted_at: a.inserted_at,
   122→            updated_at: a.updated_at
   123→          }
   124→      )
   125→
   126→    # Filter top-level artifacts (those with no parent)
   127→    top_level_artifacts =
   128→      Enum.filter(all_artifacts, fn artifact -> is_nil(artifact.artifact_id) end)
   129→
   130→    # Group child artifacts by their parent ID for efficient lookup
   131→    artifacts_by_parent =
   132→      all_artifacts
   133→      |> Enum.filter(fn artifact -> !is_nil(artifact.artifact_id) end)
   134→      |> Enum.group_by(fn artifact -> artifact.artifact_id end)
   135→
   136→    # Build the tree structure in memory
   137→    build_nested_structure(top_level_artifacts, artifacts_by_parent)
   138→  end
   139→
   140→  defp build_nested_structure(top_level_artifacts, artifacts_by_parent) do
   141→    # Create a map of all artifacts for quick lookup
   142→    all_artifacts = top_level_artifacts ++ List.flatten(Map.values(artifacts_by_parent))
   143→    artifacts_by_id = Map.new(all_artifacts, &{&1.id, &1})
   144→
   145→    # Recursive function to build the tree
   146→    build_tree = fn
   147→      build_tree, parent_id ->
   148→        # Get direct children of this parent
   149→        children =
   150→          artifacts_by_parent
   151→          |> Map.get(parent_id, [])
   152→          |> Enum.map(fn child -> Map.put(child, :children, []) end)
   153→
   154→        parent = Map.get(artifacts_by_id, parent_id)
   155→
   156→        children_total_size = Enum.reduce(children, 0, fn child, sum -> sum + child.size end)
   157→
   158→        children =
   159→          if !Enum.empty?(children) && children_total_size < parent.size do
   160→            missing_size = parent.size - children_total_size
   161→
   162→            unknown_child = %{
   163→              id: UUIDv7.generate(),
   164→              size: missing_size,
   165→              path: parent.path <> "/Unknown",
   166→              children: [],
   167→              artifact_type: :unknown,
   168→              shasum: UUIDv7.generate(),
   169→              artifact_id: parent.id
   170→            }
   171→
   172→            [unknown_child | children]
   173→          else
   174→            children
   175→          end
   176→
   177→        children = map_artifacts_with_collapsed(children)
   178→
   179→        # Recursively process each child
   180→        Enum.map(children, fn child ->
   181→          child_children = build_tree.(build_tree, child.id)
   182→          Map.put(child, :children, child_children)
   183→        end)
   184→    end
   185→
   186→    # Process each top-level artifact
   187→    top_level_artifacts
   188→    |> map_artifacts_with_collapsed()
   189→    |> Enum.map(fn artifact ->
   190→      children = build_tree.(build_tree, artifact.id)
   191→      Map.put(artifact, :children, children)
   192→    end)
   193→  end
   194→
   195→  defp map_artifacts_with_collapsed(artifacts) do
   196→    artifacts
   197→    |> Enum.sort_by(&(-&1.size))
   198→    |> Enum.with_index()
   199→    |> Enum.map(fn {artifact, index} ->
   200→      Map.put(artifact, :collapsed?, index >= 50)
   201→    end)
   202→  end
   203→
   204→  def install_size_deviation(%Bundle{} = bundle) do
   205→    project = Repo.preload(bundle, :project).project
   206→    last_bundle = last_project_bundle(project, git_branch: project.default_branch, bundle: bundle)
   207→
   208→    if is_nil(last_bundle) do
   209→      0.0
   210→    else
   211→      bundle.install_size / last_bundle.install_size - 1
   212→    end
   213→  end
   214→
   215→  def distinct_project_app_bundles(%Project{} = project) do
   216→    from(b in Bundle)
   217→    |> where([b], b.project_id == ^project.id)
   218→    |> where([b], b.inserted_at > ^DateTime.add(DateTime.utc_now(), -365, :day))
   219→    |> order_by([b], desc: b.inserted_at)
   220→    |> distinct([b], b.name)
   221→    |> Repo.all()
   222→    |> Enum.sort_by(fn bundle -> bundle.inserted_at end, {:desc, DateTime})
   223→  end
   224→
   225→  # credo:disable-for-next-line Credo.Check.Refactor.CyclomaticComplexity
   226→  def last_project_bundle(%Project{} = project, opts \\ []) do
   227→    query = where(from(b in Bundle), [b], b.project_id == ^project.id)
   228→
   229→    bundle = Keyword.get(opts, :bundle)
   230→
   231→    query =
   232→      if is_nil(bundle) do
   233→        query
   234→      else
   235→        where(
   236→          query,
   237→          [b],
   238→          b.id != ^bundle.id and b.app_bundle_id == ^bundle.app_bundle_id and
   239→            b.inserted_at < ^bundle.inserted_at
   240→        )
   241→      end
   242→
   243→    name = Keyword.get(opts, :name)
   244→
   245→    query =
   246→      if is_nil(name) do
   247→        query
   248→      else
   249→        where(query, [b], b.name == ^name)
   250→      end
   251→
   252→    inserted_before = Keyword.get(opts, :inserted_before)
   253→
   254→    query =
   255→      if is_nil(inserted_before) do
   256→        query
   257→      else
   258→        where(query, [b], b.inserted_at < ^inserted_before)
   259→      end
   260→
   261→    git_branch = Keyword.get(opts, :git_branch)
   262→    type = Keyword.get(opts, :type)
   263→
   264→    fallback = Keyword.get(opts, :fallback, true)
   265→
   266→    last_bundle =
   267→      query
   268→      |> then(&if(is_nil(git_branch), do: &1, else: where(&1, [b], b.git_branch == ^git_branch)))
   269→      |> then(&if(is_nil(type), do: &1, else: where(&1, [b], b.type == ^type)))
   270→      |> order_by([b], desc: b.inserted_at)
   271→      |> limit(1)
   272→      |> Repo.one()
   273→
   274→    if is_nil(last_bundle) && fallback do
   275→      query
   276→      |> then(&if(is_nil(type), do: &1, else: where(&1, [b], b.type == ^type)))
   277→      |> order_by([b], desc: b.inserted_at)
   278→      |> limit(1)
   279→      |> Repo.one()
   280→    else
   281→      last_bundle
   282→    end
   283→  end
   284→
   285→  def list_bundles(attrs, opts \\ []) do
   286→    preload = Keyword.get(opts, :preload, [])
   287→
   288→    Bundle
   289→    |> preload(^preload)
   290→    |> Flop.validate_and_run!(attrs, for: Bundle)
   291→  end
   292→
   293→  def project_bundle_install_size_analytics(%Project{} = project, opts \\ []) do
   294→    start_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :start_datetime, DateTime.add(DateTime.utc_now(), -30, :day))
   295→    end_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :end_datetime, DateTime.utc_now())
   296→    date_period = date_period(start_datetime: start_datetime, end_datetime: end_datetime)
   297→    # Group bundles by date
   298→    bundle_install_sizes =
   299→      project
   300→      |> project_bundles_by_date(opts)
   301→      |> Enum.map(fn {date, bundles} ->
   302→        # Find the latest bundle for each day
   303→        latest_bundle =
   304→          bundles
   305→          |> Enum.sort_by(fn bundle -> bundle.inserted_at end, {:desc, DateTime})
   306→          |> List.first()
   307→
   308→        # Return analytics data point
   309→        %{
   310→          date: date,
   311→          install_size: latest_bundle.install_size,
   312→          bundle_id: latest_bundle.id
   313→        }
   314→      end)
   315→      |> Enum.sort_by(fn %{date: date} -> date end)
   316→      |> Map.new(fn %{date: date, install_size: install_size} -> {date, install_size} end)
   317→
   318→    date_period
   319→    |> date_range_for_date_period(start_datetime: start_datetime, end_datetime: end_datetime)
   320→    |> Enum.map(fn date ->
   321→      average = Map.get(bundle_install_sizes, date)
   322→
   323→      %{
   324→        date: date,
   325→        bundle_install_size:
   326→          if is_nil(average) do
   327→            find_fallback_size(bundle_install_sizes, date)
   328→          else
   329→            average
   330→          end
   331→      }
   332→    end)
   333→  end
   334→
   335→  defp find_fallback_size(bundle_sizes, date) do
   336→    1..7
   337→    |> Enum.map(fn days_back ->
   338→      previous_date = Date.add(date, -days_back)
   339→      Map.get(bundle_sizes, previous_date)
   340→    end)
   341→    |> Enum.find(&(not is_nil(&1))) || 0
   342→  end
   343→
   344→  def bundle_download_size_analytics(%Project{} = project, opts \\ []) do
   345→    start_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :start_datetime, DateTime.add(DateTime.utc_now(), -30, :day))
   346→    end_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :end_datetime, DateTime.utc_now())
   347→    date_period = date_period(start_datetime: start_datetime, end_datetime: end_datetime)
   348→    # Group bundles by date
   349→    bundle_download_sizes =
   350→      project
   351→      |> project_bundles_by_date(opts)
   352→      |> Enum.map(fn {date, bundles} ->
   353→        # Find the latest bundle for each day
   354→        latest_bundle =
   355→          bundles
   356→          |> Enum.sort_by(fn bundle -> bundle.inserted_at end, {:desc, DateTime})
   357→          |> List.first()
   358→
   359→        # Return analytics data point
   360→        %{
   361→          date: date,
   362→          download_size: latest_bundle.download_size,
   363→          bundle_id: latest_bundle.id
   364→        }
   365→      end)
   366→      |> Enum.sort_by(fn %{date: date} -> date end)
   367→      |> Map.new(fn %{date: date, download_size: download_size} -> {date, download_size} end)
   368→
   369→    date_period
   370→    |> date_range_for_date_period(start_datetime: start_datetime, end_datetime: end_datetime)
   371→    |> Enum.map(fn date ->
   372→      average = Map.get(bundle_download_sizes, date)
   373→
   374→      %{
   375→        date: date,
   376→        bundle_download_size:
   377→          if is_nil(average) do
   378→            find_fallback_size(bundle_download_sizes, date)
   379→          else
   380→            average
   381→          end
   382→      }
   383→    end)
   384→  end
   385→
   386→  defp project_bundles_by_date(%Project{} = project, opts) do
   387→    start_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :start_datetime, DateTime.add(DateTime.utc_now(), -30, :day))
   388→    end_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :end_datetime, DateTime.utc_now())
   389→    git_branch = Keyword.get(opts, :git_branch)
   390→    type = Keyword.get(opts, :type)
   391→    date_period = date_period(start_datetime: start_datetime, end_datetime: end_datetime)
   392→
   393→    query =
   394→      from(b in Bundle)
   395→      |> where([b], b.project_id == ^project.id)
   396→      |> then(&if(is_nil(git_branch), do: &1, else: where(&1, [b], b.git_branch == ^git_branch)))
   397→      |> then(&if(is_nil(type), do: &1, else: where(&1, [b], b.type == ^type)))
   398→      |> select([b], %{
   399→        id: b.id,
   400→        inserted_at: b.inserted_at,
   401→        install_size: b.install_size,
   402→        download_size: b.download_size
   403→      })
   404→
   405→    query
   406→    |> Repo.all()
   407→    |> Enum.map(fn bundle ->
   408→      date =
   409→        case date_period do
   410→          :hour -> bundle.inserted_at |> DateTime.truncate(:second) |> truncate_to_hour()
   411→          :day -> DateTime.to_date(bundle.inserted_at)
   412→          :month -> bundle.inserted_at |> DateTime.to_date() |> Timex.beginning_of_month()
   413→        end
   414→
   415→      Map.put(bundle, :date, date)
   416→    end)
   417→    |> Enum.group_by(fn bundle -> bundle.date end)
   418→  end
   419→
   420→  defp truncate_to_hour(%DateTime{} = dt) do
   421→    %{dt | minute: 0, second: 0, microsecond: {0, 0}}
   422→  end
   423→
   424→  defp date_period(opts) do
   425→    start_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :start_datetime)
   426→    end_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :end_datetime)
   427→    days_delta = Date.diff(DateTime.to_date(end_datetime), DateTime.to_date(start_datetime))
   428→
   429→    cond do
   430→      days_delta <= 1 -> :hour
   431→      days_delta >= 60 -> :month
   432→      true -> :day
   433→    end
   434→  end
   435→
   436→  defp date_range_for_date_period(:hour, opts) do
   437→    start_datetime = DateTime.truncate(Keyword.get(opts, :start_datetime), :second)
   438→    end_datetime = DateTime.truncate(Keyword.get(opts, :end_datetime), :second)
   439→
   440→    start_datetime
   441→    |> Stream.iterate(&DateTime.add(&1, 1, :hour))
   442→    |> Enum.take_while(&(DateTime.compare(&1, end_datetime) != :gt))
   443→  end
   444→
   445→  defp date_range_for_date_period(date_period, opts) do
   446→    start_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :start_datetime)
   447→    end_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :end_datetime)
   448→    start_date = DateTime.to_date(start_datetime)
   449→    end_date = DateTime.to_date(end_datetime)
   450→
   451→    start_date
   452→    |> Date.range(end_date)
   453→    |> Enum.filter(fn date ->
   454→      case date_period do
   455→        :month ->
   456→          date.day == 1
   457→
   458→        :day ->
   459→          true
   460→      end
   461→    end)
   462→  end
   463→
   464→  def default_app(%Project{} = project) do
   465→    apps = distinct_project_app_bundles(project)
   466→
   467→    if Enum.empty?(apps) do
   468→      nil
   469→    else
   470→      (Enum.find(apps, &Enum.member?(&1.supported_platforms, :ios)) ||
   471→         List.first(apps)).name
   472→    end
   473→  end
   474→
   475→  def has_bundles_in_project_default_branch?(%Project{} = project) do
   476→    from(b in Bundle)
   477→    |> where([b], b.project_id == ^project.id)
   478→    |> where([b], b.git_branch == ^project.default_branch)
   479→    |> limit(1)
   480→    |> Repo.exists?()
   481→  end
   482→
   483→  def has_bundles_in_project?(%Project{} = project) do
   484→    from(b in Bundle)
   485→    |> where([b], b.project_id == ^project.id)
   486→    |> limit(1)
   487→    |> Repo.exists?()
   488→  end
   489→
   490→  def delete_bundle!(%Bundle{} = bundle) do
   491→    Repo.delete!(bundle)
   492→  end
   493→
   494→  def get_project_bundle_thresholds(%Project{} = project) do
   495→    Repo.all(from(bt in BundleThreshold, where: bt.project_id == ^project.id, order_by: [asc: bt.inserted_at]))
   496→  end
   497→
   498→  def get_bundle_threshold(id) do
   499→    case Repo.get(BundleThreshold, id) do
   500→      nil -> {:error, :not_found}
   501→      threshold -> {:ok, threshold}
   502→    end
   503→  end
   504→
   505→  def create_bundle_threshold(attrs) do
   506→    %BundleThreshold{id: UUIDv7.generate()}
   507→    |> BundleThreshold.changeset(attrs)
   508→    |> Repo.insert()
   509→  end
   510→
   511→  def update_bundle_threshold(%BundleThreshold{} = threshold, attrs) do
   512→    threshold
   513→    |> BundleThreshold.changeset(attrs)
   514→    |> Repo.update()
   515→  end
   516→
   517→  def delete_bundle_threshold(%BundleThreshold{} = threshold) do
   518→    Repo.delete(threshold)
   519→  end
   520→
   521→  def evaluate_project_thresholds(%Project{} = project, %Bundle{} = bundle) do
   522→    thresholds = get_project_bundle_thresholds(project)
   523→
   524→    Enum.reduce_while(thresholds, :ok, fn threshold, :ok ->
   525→      case evaluate_single_threshold(project, bundle, threshold) do
   526→        :ok -> {:cont, :ok}
   527→        {:violated, _, _} = violation -> {:halt, violation}
   528→      end
   529→    end)
   530→  end
   531→
   532→  defp evaluate_single_threshold(project, bundle, threshold) do
   533→    if threshold.bundle_name && threshold.bundle_name != bundle.name do
   534→      :ok
   535→    else
   536→      baseline =
   537→        last_project_bundle(project,
   538→          git_branch: threshold.baseline_branch,
   539→          name: bundle.name,
   540→          fallback: false
   541→        )
   542→
   543→      check_threshold_deviation(threshold, bundle, baseline)
   544→    end
   545→  end
   546→
   547→  defp check_threshold_deviation(_threshold, _bundle, nil), do: :ok
   548→
   549→  defp check_threshold_deviation(threshold, bundle, baseline) do
   550→    {current_size, baseline_size} =
   551→      case threshold.metric do
   552→        :install_size -> {bundle.install_size, baseline.install_size}
   553→        :download_size -> {bundle.download_size, baseline.download_size}
   554→      end
   555→
   556→    if is_nil(current_size) || is_nil(baseline_size) || baseline_size == 0 do
   557→      :ok
   558→    else
   559→      deviation = (current_size - baseline_size) / baseline_size * 100
   560→
   561→      if deviation > threshold.deviation_percentage do
   562→        {:violated, threshold, %{current_size: current_size, baseline_size: baseline_size, deviation: deviation}}
   563→      else
   564→        :ok
   565→      end
   566→    end
   567→  end
   568→
   569→  defp flatten_artifacts(
   570→         artifacts,
   571→         bundle_id,
   572→         parent_id \\ nil,
   573→         current_timestamp \\ DateTime.truncate(DateTime.utc_now(), :second)
   574→       ) do
   575→    valid_artifact_types =
   576→      Artifact |> Ecto.Enum.values(:artifact_type) |> Enum.map(&Atom.to_string/1)
   577→
   578→    Enum.flat_map(artifacts, fn artifact ->
   579→      artifact_id = UUIDv7.generate()
   580→      artifact_type = Map.get(artifact, :artifact_type)
   581→
   582→      if !Enum.member?(valid_artifact_types, Atom.to_string(artifact_type)) do
   583→        raise "Invalid artifact type: #{artifact_type}. Must be one of #{inspect(valid_artifact_types)}."
   584→      end
   585→
   586→      current_artifact = %{
   587→        id: artifact_id,
   588→        artifact_type: Map.get(artifact, :artifact_type),
   589→        path: Map.get(artifact, :path),
   590→        size: Map.get(artifact, :size),
   591→        shasum: Map.get(artifact, :shasum),
   592→        bundle_id: bundle_id,
   593→        artifact_id: parent_id,
   594→        inserted_at: current_timestamp,
   595→        updated_at: current_timestamp
   596→      }
   597→
   598→      children = Map.get(artifact, :children) || []
   599→      child_artifacts = flatten_artifacts(children, bundle_id, artifact_id, current_timestamp)
   600→
   601→      [current_artifact | child_artifacts]
   602→    end)
   603→  end
   604→end
   605→

defmodule Tuist.Bundles do
  @moduledoc """
  The Bundles context.
  """

  import Ecto.Query

  alias Tuist.Bundles.Artifact
  alias Tuist.Bundles.Bundle
  alias Tuist.Bundles.BundleThreshold
  alias Tuist.Projects.Project
  alias Tuist.Repo

  @doc """
  Creates a bundle with associated artifacts.
  """
  def create_bundle(attrs \\ %{}, opts \\ []) do
    {artifacts, bundle_attrs} = Map.pop(attrs, :artifacts, [])
    bundle_id = Map.fetch!(attrs, :id)

    Ecto.Multi.new()
    |> create_bundle_multi(bundle_attrs)
    |> create_artifacts_multi(artifacts, bundle_id)
    |> execute_bundle_transaction(opts)
  end

  defp create_bundle_multi(multi, bundle_attrs) do
    Ecto.Multi.insert(multi, :bundle, Bundle.changeset(%Bundle{}, bundle_attrs))
  end

  defp create_artifacts_multi(multi, artifacts, bundle_id) do
    Ecto.Multi.run(multi, :artifacts, fn repo, _changes ->
      insert_artifacts_in_batches(repo, artifacts, bundle_id)
    end)
  end

  defp execute_bundle_transaction(multi, opts) do
    preload = Keyword.get(opts, :preload, [])

    case Repo.transaction(multi) do
      {:ok, %{bundle: bundle}} -> {:ok, Repo.preload(bundle, preload)}
      {:error, _operation, changeset, _changes} -> {:error, changeset}
    end
  end

  defp insert_artifacts_in_batches(repo, artifacts, bundle_id) do
    artifacts
    |> flatten_artifacts(bundle_id)
    |> batch_insert_artifacts(repo)
  end

  defp batch_insert_artifacts(flattened_artifacts, repo) do
    # Each artifact has ~10 fields, so 6000 artifacts per batch keeps us under 65535 params
    batch_size = 6000

    flattened_artifacts
    |> Enum.chunk_every(batch_size)
    |> Enum.reduce_while({:ok, []}, fn batch, {:ok, acc} ->
      case repo.insert_all(Artifact, batch, returning: true) do
        {_count, artifacts} -> {:cont, {:ok, acc ++ artifacts}}
        error -> {:halt, error}
      end
    end)
  end

  @doc """
  Gets a single bundle.
  """
  def get_bundle(id, opts \\ []) do
    preload = Keyword.get(opts, :preload, [])
    project_id = Keyword.get(opts, :project_id)

    query =
      then(
        from(b in Bundle, where: b.id == ^id, preload: ^preload),
        &if(is_nil(project_id), do: &1, else: where(&1, [b], b.project_id == ^project_id))
      )

    bundle = Repo.one(query)

    if is_nil(bundle) do
      {:error, :not_found}
    else
      {:ok, %{bundle | artifacts: bundle_artifacts(bundle)}}
    end
  end

  def list_bundle_artifacts(bundle_id, opts \\ []) do
    parent_artifact_id = Keyword.get(opts, :parent_artifact_id)

    query =
      from(a in Artifact,
        where: a.bundle_id == ^bundle_id,
        order_by: [asc: a.path]
      )

    query =
      if parent_artifact_id do
        where(query, [a], a.artifact_id == ^parent_artifact_id)
      else
        where(query, [a], is_nil(a.artifact_id))
      end

    Repo.all(query)
  end

  defp bundle_artifacts(%Bundle{id: id}) do
    # Get all artifacts for this bundle in a single query
    all_artifacts =
      Repo.all(
        from a in Artifact,
          where: a.bundle_id == ^id,
          select: %{
            id: a.id,
            artifact_type: a.artifact_type,
            path: a.path,
            size: a.size,
            shasum: a.shasum,
            artifact_id: a.artifact_id,
            bundle_id: a.bundle_id,
            inserted_at: a.inserted_at,
            updated_at: a.updated_at
          }
      )

    # Filter top-level artifacts (those with no parent)
    top_level_artifacts =
      Enum.filter(all_artifacts, fn artifact -> is_nil(artifact.artifact_id) end)

    # Group child artifacts by their parent ID for efficient lookup
    artifacts_by_parent =
      all_artifacts
      |> Enum.filter(fn artifact -> !is_nil(artifact.artifact_id) end)
      |> Enum.group_by(fn artifact -> artifact.artifact_id end)

    # Build the tree structure in memory
    build_nested_structure(top_level_artifacts, artifacts_by_parent)
  end

  defp build_nested_structure(top_level_artifacts, artifacts_by_parent) do
    # Create a map of all artifacts for quick lookup
    all_artifacts = top_level_artifacts ++ List.flatten(Map.values(artifacts_by_parent))
    artifacts_by_id = Map.new(all_artifacts, &{&1.id, &1})

    # Recursive function to build the tree
    build_tree = fn
      build_tree, parent_id ->
        # Get direct children of this parent
        children =
          artifacts_by_parent
          |> Map.get(parent_id, [])
          |> Enum.map(fn child -> Map.put(child, :children, []) end)

        parent = Map.get(artifacts_by_id, parent_id)

        children_total_size = Enum.reduce(children, 0, fn child, sum -> sum + child.size end)

        children =
          if !Enum.empty?(children) && children_total_size < parent.size do
            missing_size = parent.size - children_total_size

            unknown_child = %{
              id: UUIDv7.generate(),
              size: missing_size,
              path: parent.path <> "/Unknown",
              children: [],
              artifact_type: :unknown,
              shasum: UUIDv7.generate(),
              artifact_id: parent.id
            }

            [unknown_child | children]
          else
            children
          end

        children = map_artifacts_with_collapsed(children)

        # Recursively process each child
        Enum.map(children, fn child ->
          child_children = build_tree.(build_tree, child.id)
          Map.put(child, :children, child_children)
        end)
    end

    # Process each top-level artifact
    top_level_artifacts
    |> map_artifacts_with_collapsed()
    |> Enum.map(fn artifact ->
      children = build_tree.(build_tree, artifact.id)
      Map.put(artifact, :children, children)
    end)
  end

  defp map_artifacts_with_collapsed(artifacts) do
    artifacts
    |> Enum.sort_by(&(-&1.size))
    |> Enum.with_index()
    |> Enum.map(fn {artifact, index} ->
      Map.put(artifact, :collapsed?, index >= 50)
    end)
  end

  def install_size_deviation(%Bundle{} = bundle) do
    project = Repo.preload(bundle, :project).project
    last_bundle = last_project_bundle(project, git_branch: project.default_branch, bundle: bundle)

    if is_nil(last_bundle) do
      0.0
    else
      bundle.install_size / last_bundle.install_size - 1
    end
  end

  def distinct_project_app_bundles(%Project{} = project) do
    from(b in Bundle)
    |> where([b], b.project_id == ^project.id)
    |> where([b], b.inserted_at > ^DateTime.add(DateTime.utc_now(), -365, :day))
    |> order_by([b], desc: b.inserted_at)
    |> distinct([b], b.name)
    |> Repo.all()
    |> Enum.sort_by(fn bundle -> bundle.inserted_at end, {:desc, DateTime})
  end

  # credo:disable-for-next-line Credo.Check.Refactor.CyclomaticComplexity
  def last_project_bundle(%Project{} = project, opts \\ []) do
    query = where(from(b in Bundle), [b], b.project_id == ^project.id)

    bundle = Keyword.get(opts, :bundle)

    query =
      if is_nil(bundle) do
        query
      else
        where(
          query,
          [b],
          b.id != ^bundle.id and b.app_bundle_id == ^bundle.app_bundle_id and
            b.inserted_at < ^bundle.inserted_at
        )
      end

    name = Keyword.get(opts, :name)

    query =
      if is_nil(name) do
        query
      else
        where(query, [b], b.name == ^name)
      end

    inserted_before = Keyword.get(opts, :inserted_before)

    query =
      if is_nil(inserted_before) do
        query
      else
        where(query, [b], b.inserted_at < ^inserted_before)
      end

    git_branch = Keyword.get(opts, :git_branch)
    type = Keyword.get(opts, :type)

    fallback = Keyword.get(opts, :fallback, true)

    last_bundle =
      query
      |> then(&if(is_nil(git_branch), do: &1, else: where(&1, [b], b.git_branch == ^git_branch)))
      |> then(&if(is_nil(type), do: &1, else: where(&1, [b], b.type == ^type)))
      |> order_by([b], desc: b.inserted_at)
      |> limit(1)
      |> Repo.one()

    if is_nil(last_bundle) && fallback do
      query
      |> then(&if(is_nil(type), do: &1, else: where(&1, [b], b.type == ^type)))
      |> order_by([b], desc: b.inserted_at)
      |> limit(1)
      |> Repo.one()
    else
      last_bundle
    end
  end

  def list_bundles(attrs, opts \\ []) do
    preload = Keyword.get(opts, :preload, [])

    Bundle
    |> preload(^preload)
    |> Flop.validate_and_run!(attrs, for: Bundle)
  end

  def project_bundle_install_size_analytics(%Project{} = project, opts \\ []) do
    start_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :start_datetime, DateTime.add(DateTime.utc_now(), -30, :day))
    end_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :end_datetime, DateTime.utc_now())
    date_period = date_period(start_datetime: start_datetime, end_datetime: end_datetime)
    # Group bundles by date
    bundle_install_sizes =
      project
      |> project_bundles_by_date(opts)
      |> Enum.map(fn {date, bundles} ->
        # Find the latest bundle for each day
        latest_bundle =
          bundles
          |> Enum.sort_by(fn bundle -> bundle.inserted_at end, {:desc, DateTime})
          |> List.first()

        # Return analytics data point
        %{
          date: date,
          install_size: latest_bundle.install_size,
          bundle_id: latest_bundle.id
        }
      end)
      |> Enum.sort_by(fn %{date: date} -> date end)
      |> Map.new(fn %{date: date, install_size: install_size} -> {date, install_size} end)

    date_period
    |> date_range_for_date_period(start_datetime: start_datetime, end_datetime: end_datetime)
    |> Enum.map(fn date ->
      average = Map.get(bundle_install_sizes, date)

      %{
        date: date,
        bundle_install_size:
          if is_nil(average) do
            find_fallback_size(bundle_install_sizes, date)
          else
            average
          end
      }
    end)
  end

  defp find_fallback_size(bundle_sizes, date) do
    1..7
    |> Enum.map(fn days_back ->
      previous_date = Date.add(date, -days_back)
      Map.get(bundle_sizes, previous_date)
    end)
    |> Enum.find(&(not is_nil(&1))) || 0
  end

  def bundle_download_size_analytics(%Project{} = project, opts \\ []) do
    start_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :start_datetime, DateTime.add(DateTime.utc_now(), -30, :day))
    end_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :end_datetime, DateTime.utc_now())
    date_period = date_period(start_datetime: start_datetime, end_datetime: end_datetime)
    # Group bundles by date
    bundle_download_sizes =
      project
      |> project_bundles_by_date(opts)
      |> Enum.map(fn {date, bundles} ->
        # Find the latest bundle for each day
        latest_bundle =
          bundles
          |> Enum.sort_by(fn bundle -> bundle.inserted_at end, {:desc, DateTime})
          |> List.first()

        # Return analytics data point
        %{
          date: date,
          download_size: latest_bundle.download_size,
          bundle_id: latest_bundle.id
        }
      end)
      |> Enum.sort_by(fn %{date: date} -> date end)
      |> Map.new(fn %{date: date, download_size: download_size} -> {date, download_size} end)

    date_period
    |> date_range_for_date_period(start_datetime: start_datetime, end_datetime: end_datetime)
    |> Enum.map(fn date ->
      average = Map.get(bundle_download_sizes, date)

      %{
        date: date,
        bundle_download_size:
          if is_nil(average) do
            find_fallback_size(bundle_download_sizes, date)
          else
            average
          end
      }
    end)
  end

  defp project_bundles_by_date(%Project{} = project, opts) do
    start_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :start_datetime, DateTime.add(DateTime.utc_now(), -30, :day))
    end_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :end_datetime, DateTime.utc_now())
    git_branch = Keyword.get(opts, :git_branch)
    type = Keyword.get(opts, :type)
    date_period = date_period(start_datetime: start_datetime, end_datetime: end_datetime)

    query =
      from(b in Bundle)
      |> where([b], b.project_id == ^project.id)
      |> then(&if(is_nil(git_branch), do: &1, else: where(&1, [b], b.git_branch == ^git_branch)))
      |> then(&if(is_nil(type), do: &1, else: where(&1, [b], b.type == ^type)))
      |> select([b], %{
        id: b.id,
        inserted_at: b.inserted_at,
        install_size: b.install_size,
        download_size: b.download_size
      })

    query
    |> Repo.all()
    |> Enum.map(fn bundle ->
      date =
        case date_period do
          :hour -> bundle.inserted_at |> DateTime.truncate(:second) |> truncate_to_hour()
          :day -> DateTime.to_date(bundle.inserted_at)
          :month -> bundle.inserted_at |> DateTime.to_date() |> Timex.beginning_of_month()
        end

      Map.put(bundle, :date, date)
    end)
    |> Enum.group_by(fn bundle -> bundle.date end)
  end

  defp truncate_to_hour(%DateTime{} = dt) do
    %{dt | minute: 0, second: 0, microsecond: {0, 0}}
  end

  defp date_period(opts) do
    start_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :start_datetime)
    end_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :end_datetime)
    days_delta = Date.diff(DateTime.to_date(end_datetime), DateTime.to_date(start_datetime))

    cond do
      days_delta <= 1 -> :hour
      days_delta >= 60 -> :month
      true -> :day
    end
  end

  defp date_range_for_date_period(:hour, opts) do
    start_datetime = DateTime.truncate(Keyword.get(opts, :start_datetime), :second)
    end_datetime = DateTime.truncate(Keyword.get(opts, :end_datetime), :second)

    start_datetime
    |> Stream.iterate(&DateTime.add(&1, 1, :hour))
    |> Enum.take_while(&(DateTime.compare(&1, end_datetime) != :gt))
  end

  defp date_range_for_date_period(date_period, opts) do
    start_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :start_datetime)
    end_datetime = Keyword.get(opts, :end_datetime)
    start_date = DateTime.to_date(start_datetime)
    end_date = DateTime.to_date(end_datetime)

    start_date
    |> Date.range(end_date)
    |> Enum.filter(fn date ->
      case date_period do
        :month ->
          date.day == 1

        :day ->
          true
      end
    end)
  end

  def default_app(%Project{} = project) do
    apps = distinct_project_app_bundles(project)

    if Enum.empty?(apps) do
      nil
    else
      (Enum.find(apps, &Enum.member?(&1.supported_platforms, :ios)) ||
         List.first(apps)).name
    end
  end

  def has_bundles_in_project_default_branch?(%Project{} = project) do
    from(b in Bundle)
    |> where([b], b.project_id == ^project.id)
    |> where([b], b.git_branch == ^project.default_branch)
    |> limit(1)
    |> Repo.exists?()
  end

  def has_bundles_in_project?(%Project{} = project) do
    from(b in Bundle)
    |> where([b], b.project_id == ^project.id)
    |> limit(1)
    |> Repo.exists?()
  end

  def delete_bundle!(%Bundle{} = bundle) do
    Repo.delete!(bundle)
  end

  def get_project_bundle_thresholds(%Project{} = project) do
    Repo.all(from(bt in BundleThreshold, where: bt.project_id == ^project.id, order_by: [asc: bt.inserted_at]))
  end

  def get_bundle_threshold(id) do
    case Repo.get(BundleThreshold, id) do
      nil -> {:error, :not_found}
      threshold -> {:ok, threshold}
    end
  end

  def create_bundle_threshold(attrs) do
    %BundleThreshold{id: UUIDv7.generate()}
    |> BundleThreshold.changeset(attrs)
    |> Repo.insert()
  end

  def update_bundle_threshold(%BundleThreshold{} = threshold, attrs) do
    threshold
    |> BundleThreshold.changeset(attrs)
    |> Repo.update()
  end

  def delete_bundle_threshold(%BundleThreshold{} = threshold) do
    Repo.delete(threshold)
  end

  def evaluate_project_thresholds(%Project{} = project, %Bundle{} = bundle) do
    thresholds = get_project_bundle_thresholds(project)

    Enum.reduce_while(thresholds, :ok, fn threshold, :ok ->
      case evaluate_single_threshold(project, bundle, threshold) do
        :ok -> {:cont, :ok}
        {:violated, _, _} = violation -> {:halt, violation}
      end
    end)
  end

  defp evaluate_single_threshold(project, bundle, threshold) do
    if threshold.bundle_name && threshold.bundle_name != bundle.name do
      :ok
    else
      baseline =
        last_project_bundle(project,
          git_branch: threshold.baseline_branch,
          name: bundle.name,
          fallback: false
        )

      check_threshold_deviation(threshold, bundle, baseline)
    end
  end

  defp check_threshold_deviation(_threshold, _bundle, nil), do: :ok

  defp check_threshold_deviation(threshold, bundle, baseline) do
    {current_size, baseline_size} =
      case threshold.metric do
        :install_size -> {bundle.install_size, baseline.install_size}
        :download_size -> {bundle.download_size, baseline.download_size}
      end

    if is_nil(current_size) || is_nil(baseline_size) || baseline_size == 0 do
      :ok
    else
      deviation = (current_size - baseline_size) / baseline_size * 100

      if deviation > threshold.deviation_percentage do
        {:violated, threshold, %{current_size: current_size, baseline_size: baseline_size, deviation: deviation}}
      else
        :ok
      end
    end
  end

  defp flatten_artifacts(
         artifacts,
         bundle_id,
         parent_id \\ nil,
         current_timestamp \\ DateTime.truncate(DateTime.utc_now(), :second)
       ) do
    valid_artifact_types =
      Artifact |> Ecto.Enum.values(:artifact_type) |> Enum.map(&Atom.to_string/1)

    Enum.flat_map(artifacts, fn artifact ->
      artifact_id = UUIDv7.generate()
      artifact_type = Map.get(artifact, :artifact_type)

      if !Enum.member?(valid_artifact_types, Atom.to_string(artifact_type)) do
        raise "Invalid artifact type: #{artifact_type}. Must be one of #{inspect(valid_artifact_types)}."
      end

      current_artifact = %{
        id: artifact_id,
        artifact_type: Map.get(artifact, :artifact_type),
        path: Map.get(artifact, :path),
        size: Map.get(artifact, :size),
        shasum: Map.get(artifact, :shasum),
        bundle_id: bundle_id,
        artifact_id: parent_id,
        inserted_at: current_timestamp,
        updated_at: current_timestamp
      }

      children = Map.get(artifact, :children) || []
      child_artifacts = flatten_artifacts(children, bundle_id, artifact_id, current_timestamp)

      [current_artifact | child_artifacts]
    end)
  end
end

   227→  defp message(error), do: Map.get(error.data, :message) || Map.get(error.data, "message")
   228→end

defmodule Tuist.MCP.Server do
  @moduledoc false

  use Anubis.Server,
    name: "tuist",
    version: "1.4.1",
    capabilities: [
      {:tools, list_changed?: false},
      {:prompts, list_changed?: false}
    ],
    protocol_versions: ["2025-03-26"]

  alias Anubis.MCP.Error
  alias Anubis.Server.Frame
  alias Anubis.Server.Handlers
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareBuilds
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareBundles
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareCacheRuns
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareGenerations
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareTestCase
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.CompareTestRuns
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Prompts.FixFlakyTest
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetBundle
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetCacheRun
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetGeneration
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetTestCase
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetTestCaseRun
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetTestCaseRunAttachment
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetTestRun
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.GetXcodeBuild
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListBundleArtifacts
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListBundles
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListCacheRuns
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListGenerations
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListProjects
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestCaseRunAttachments
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestCaseRuns
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestCases
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestModuleRuns
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestRuns
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListTestSuiteRuns
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListXcodeBuildCacheTasks
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListXcodeBuildCASOutputs
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListXcodeBuildFiles
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListXcodeBuildIssues
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListXcodeBuilds
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListXcodeBuildTargets
  alias Tuist.MCP.Components.Tools.ListXcodeModuleCacheTargets

  # Xcode build tools
  component(ListXcodeBuilds, name: "list_xcode_builds")
  component(GetXcodeBuild, name: "get_xcode_build")
  component(ListXcodeBuildTargets, name: "list_xcode_build_targets")
  component(ListXcodeBuildFiles, name: "list_xcode_build_files")
  component(ListXcodeBuildIssues, name: "list_xcode_build_issues")
  component(ListXcodeBuildCacheTasks, name: "list_xcode_build_cache_tasks")
  component(ListXcodeBuildCASOutputs, name: "list_xcode_build_cas_outputs")

  # Test tools
  component(ListTestRuns, name: "list_test_runs")
  component(ListTestModuleRuns, name: "list_test_module_runs")
  component(ListTestSuiteRuns, name: "list_test_suite_runs")
  component(ListTestCaseRuns, name: "list_test_case_runs")
  component(ListTestCases, name: "list_test_cases")
  component(GetTestCase, name: "get_test_case")
  component(GetTestRun, name: "get_test_run")
  component(GetTestCaseRun, name: "get_test_case_run")
  component(ListTestCaseRunAttachments, name: "list_test_case_run_attachments")
  component(GetTestCaseRunAttachment, name: "get_test_case_run_attachment")

  # Bundle tools
  component(ListBundles, name: "list_bundles")
  component(GetBundle, name: "get_bundle")
  component(ListBundleArtifacts, name: "list_bundle_artifacts")

  # Generation tools
  component(ListGenerations, name: "list_generations")
  component(GetGeneration, name: "get_generation")

  # Cache run tools
  component(ListCacheRuns, name: "list_cache_runs")
  component(GetCacheRun, name: "get_cache_run")

  # Module cache tools
  component(ListXcodeModuleCacheTargets, name: "list_xcode_module_cache_targets")

  # Project tools
  component(ListProjects, name: "list_projects")

  # Prompts
  component(FixFlakyTest, name: "fix_flaky_test")
  component(CompareBuilds, name: "compare_builds")
  component(CompareTestRuns, name: "compare_test_runs")
  component(CompareBundles, name: "compare_bundles")
  component(CompareTestCase, name: "compare_test_case")
  component(CompareGenerations, name: "compare_generations")
  component(CompareCacheRuns, name: "compare_cache_runs")

  @impl Anubis.Server
  def handle_request(%{"method" => "tools/call", "params" => %{"name" => _name} = params} = request, %Frame{} = frame) do
    request = put_in(request, ["params"], Map.put_new(params, "arguments", %{}))
    Handlers.handle(request, __MODULE__, frame)
  end

  @impl Anubis.Server
  def handle_request(%{"method" => "tools/call"}, %Frame{} = frame) do
    {:error, invalid_params_error("Missing required parameter: name."), frame}
  end

  @impl Anubis.Server
  def handle_request(%{"method" => "prompts/get", "params" => %{"name" => _name} = params} = request, %Frame{} = frame) do
    request = put_in(request, ["params"], Map.put_new(params, "arguments", %{}))
    Handlers.handle(request, __MODULE__, frame)
  end

  @impl Anubis.Server
  def handle_request(%{"method" => "prompts/get"}, %Frame{} = frame) do
    {:error, invalid_params_error("Missing required parameter: name."), frame}
  end

  @impl Anubis.Server
  def handle_request(request, %Frame{} = frame), do: Handlers.handle(request, __MODULE__, frame)

  defp invalid_params_error(message) do
    Error.protocol(:invalid_params, %{message: message})
  end
end
